title: A Poet's Epitaph
content:
Life was unkind to him;
    All things went wrong:
    Fortune assigned to him
    Merely a song.
    Ever a mystery
    Here to his heart;
    In his life's history
    Love played no part.
    Carve on the granite,
    There at the end,
    Where all may scan it,
    Death was his friend.
    Giving him all he missed
    Here upon Earth
    Love and the call he missed
    All that was worth.
meaning:
"A Poet's Epitaph" is a quintessential example of Madison Julius Cawein's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
